Detailed Description

Macro Definition Documentation

◆ CAN_MCR_MAXMB [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_MAXMB ( x)

MAXMB - Number of the Last Message Buffer

◆ CAN_MCR_IDAM [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_IDAM ( x)

IDAM - ID Acceptance Mode 0b00..Format A: One full ID (standard and extended) per ID filter table element. 0b01..Format B: Two full standard IDs or two partial 14-bit (standard and extended) IDs per ID filter table element. 0b10..Format C: Four partial 8-bit standard IDs per ID filter table element. 0b11..Format D: All frames rejected.

◆ CAN_MCR_FDEN [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_FDEN ( x)

FDEN - CAN FD Operation Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_AEN [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_AEN ( x)

AEN - Abort Enable 0b0..Disabled 0b1..Enabled

◆ CAN_MCR_LPRIOEN [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_LPRIOEN ( x)

LPRIOEN - Local Priority Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_PNET_EN [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_PNET_EN ( x)

PNET_EN - Pretended Networking Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_DMA [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_DMA ( x)

DMA - DMA Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_IRMQ [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_IRMQ ( x)

IRMQ - Individual RX Masking and Queue Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_SRXDIS [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_SRXDIS ( x)

SRXDIS - Self-Reception Disable 0b0..Enable 0b1..Disable

◆ CAN_MCR_DOZE [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_DOZE ( x)

DOZE - Doze Mode Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_WAKSRC [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_WAKSRC ( x)

WAKSRC - Wake-Up Source 0b0..No filter applied 0b1..Filter applied

◆ CAN_MCR_LPMACK [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_LPMACK ( x)

LPMACK - Low-Power Mode Acknowledge 0b0..Not in a low-power mode 0b1..In a low-power mode

◆ CAN_MCR_WRNEN [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_WRNEN ( x)

WRNEN - Warning Interrupt Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_SLFWAK [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_SLFWAK ( x)

SLFWAK - Self-Wake-Up Feature 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_SUPV [1/2]

#define CAN_MCR_SUPV ( x)

SUPV - Supervisor Mode 0b0..User mode 0b1..Supervisor mode

◆ CAN_MCR_FRZACK [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_FRZACK ( x)

FRZACK - Freeze Mode Acknowledge 0b0..Not in Freeze mode, prescaler running. 0b1..In Freeze mode, prescaler stopped.

◆ CAN_MCR_SOFTRST [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_SOFTRST ( x)

SOFTRST - Soft Reset 0b0..No reset 0b1..Soft reset affects reset registers

◆ CAN_MCR_WAKMSK [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_WAKMSK ( x)

WAKMSK - Wake-up Interrupt Mask 0b0..Disabled 0b1..Enabled

◆ CAN_MCR_NOTRDY [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_NOTRDY ( x)

NOTRDY - FlexCAN Not Ready 0b0..FlexCAN is in Normal mode, Listen-Only mode, or Loopback mode. 0b1..FlexCAN is in Disable mode, Doze mode, Stop mode, or Freeze mode.

◆ CAN_MCR_HALT [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_HALT ( x)

HALT - Halt FlexCAN 0b0..No request 0b1..Enter Freeze mode, if MCR[FRZ] = 1.

◆ CAN_MCR_RFEN [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_RFEN ( x)

RFEN - Legacy RX FIFO Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_FRZ [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_FRZ ( x)

FRZ - Freeze Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_MDIS [1/3]

#define CAN_MCR_MDIS ( x)

MDIS - Module Disable 0b0..Enable 0b1..Disable

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PROPSEG [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PROPSEG ( x)

PROPSEG - Propagation Segment

◆ CAN_CTRL1_LOM [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_LOM ( x)

LOM - Listen-Only Mode 0b0..Listen-Only mode is deactivated. 0b1..FlexCAN module operates in Listen-Only mode.

◆ CAN_CTRL1_LBUF [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_LBUF ( x)

LBUF - Lowest Buffer Transmitted First 0b0..Buffer with highest priority is transmitted first. 0b1..Lowest number buffer is transmitted first.

◆ CAN_CTRL1_TSYN [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_TSYN ( x)

TSYN - Timer Sync 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_CTRL1_BOFFREC [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_BOFFREC ( x)

BOFFREC - Bus Off Recovery 0b0..Enabled 0b1..Disabled

◆ CAN_CTRL1_SMP [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_SMP ( x)

SMP - CAN Bit Sampling 0b0..One sample is used to determine the bit value. 0b1..Three samples are used to determine the value of the received bit: the regular one (sample point) and two preceding samples. A majority rule is used.

◆ CAN_CTRL1_RWRNMSK [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_RWRNMSK ( x)

RWRNMSK - RX Warning Interrupt Mask 0b0..Disabled 0b1..Enabled

◆ CAN_CTRL1_TWRNMSK [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_TWRNMSK ( x)

TWRNMSK - TX Warning Interrupt Mask 0b0..Disabled 0b1..Enabled

◆ CAN_CTRL1_LPB [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_LPB ( x)

LPB - Loopback Mode 0b0..Disabled 0b1..Enabled

◆ CAN_CTRL1_ERRMSK [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_ERRMSK ( x)

ERRMSK - Error Interrupt Mask 0b0..Interrupt disabled 0b1..Interrupt enabled

◆ CAN_CTRL1_BOFFMSK [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_BOFFMSK ( x)

BOFFMSK - Bus Off Interrupt Mask 0b0..Interrupt disabled 0b1..Interrupt enabled

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PSEG2 [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PSEG2 ( x)

PSEG2 - Phase Segment 2

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PSEG1 [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PSEG1 ( x)

PSEG1 - Phase Segment 1

◆ CAN_CTRL1_RJW [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_RJW ( x)

RJW - Resync Jump Width

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PRESDIV [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PRESDIV ( x)

PRESDIV - Prescaler Division Factor

◆ CAN_TIMER_TIMER [1/3]

#define CAN_TIMER_TIMER ( x)

TIMER - Timer Value

◆ CAN_RXMGMASK_MG [1/3]

#define CAN_RXMGMASK_MG ( x)

MG - Global Mask for RX Message Buffers

◆ CAN_RX14MASK_RX14M [1/3]

#define CAN_RX14MASK_RX14M ( x)

RX14M - RX Buffer 14 Mask Bits

◆ CAN_RX15MASK_RX15M [1/3]

#define CAN_RX15MASK_RX15M ( x)

RX15M - RX Buffer 15 Mask Bits

◆ CAN_ECR_TXERRCNT [1/3]

#define CAN_ECR_TXERRCNT ( x)

TXERRCNT - Transmit Error Counter

◆ CAN_ECR_RXERRCNT [1/3]

#define CAN_ECR_RXERRCNT ( x)

RXERRCNT - Receive Error Counter

◆ CAN_ECR_TXERRCNT_FAST [1/3]

#define CAN_ECR_TXERRCNT_FAST ( x)

TXERRCNT_FAST - Transmit Error Counter for Fast Bits

◆ CAN_ECR_RXERRCNT_FAST [1/3]

#define CAN_ECR_RXERRCNT_FAST ( x)

RXERRCNT_FAST - Receive Error Counter for Fast Bits

◆ CAN_ESR1_WAKINT [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_WAKINT ( x)

WAKINT - Wake-up Interrupt Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..Indicates that a recessive-to-dominant transition was received on the CAN bus.

◆ CAN_ESR1_ERRINT [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_ERRINT ( x)

ERRINT - Error Interrupt Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..Indicates setting of any error flag in the Error and Status register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_BOFFINT [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_BOFFINT ( x)

BOFFINT - Bus Off Interrupt Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..FlexCAN module entered Bus Off state.

◆ CAN_ESR1_RX [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_RX ( x)

RX - FlexCAN in Reception Flag 0b0..Not receiving 0b1..Receiving

◆ CAN_ESR1_FLTCONF [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_FLTCONF ( x)

FLTCONF - Fault Confinement State 0b00..Error Active 0b01..Error Passive 0b1x..Bus Off

◆ CAN_ESR1_TX [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_TX ( x)

TX - FlexCAN In Transmission 0b0..Not transmitting 0b1..Transmitting

◆ CAN_ESR1_IDLE [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_IDLE ( x)

IDLE - Idle 0b0..Not IDLE 0b1..IDLE

◆ CAN_ESR1_RXWRN [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_RXWRN ( x)

RXWRN - RX Error Warning Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..RXERRCNT is greater than or equal to 96.

◆ CAN_ESR1_TXWRN [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_TXWRN ( x)

TXWRN - TX Error Warning Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..TXERRCNT is 96 or greater.

◆ CAN_ESR1_STFERR [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_STFERR ( x)

STFERR - Stuffing Error Flag 0b0..No error 0b1..Error occurred since last read of this register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_FRMERR [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_FRMERR ( x)

FRMERR - Form Error Flag 0b0..No error 0b1..Error occurred since last read of this register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_CRCERR [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_CRCERR ( x)

CRCERR - Cyclic Redundancy Check Error Flag 0b0..No error 0b1..Error occurred since last read of this register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_ACKERR [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_ACKERR ( x)

ACKERR - Acknowledge Error Flag 0b0..No error 0b1..Error occurred since last read of this register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_BIT0ERR [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_BIT0ERR ( x)

BIT0ERR - Bit0 Error Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..At least one bit sent as dominant is received as recessive.

◆ CAN_ESR1_BIT1ERR [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_BIT1ERR ( x)

BIT1ERR - Bit1 Error Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..At least one bit sent as recessive is received as dominant.

◆ CAN_ESR1_RWRNINT [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_RWRNINT ( x)

RWRNINT - RX Warning Interrupt Flag 0b0..No such occurrence 0b1..RX error counter changed from less than 96 to greater than or equal to 96.

◆ CAN_ESR1_TWRNINT [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_TWRNINT ( x)

TWRNINT - TX Warning Interrupt Flag 0b0..No such occurrence 0b1..TX error counter changed from less than 96 to greater than or equal to 96.

◆ CAN_ESR1_SYNCH [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_SYNCH ( x)

SYNCH - CAN Synchronization Status Flag 0b0..Not synchronized 0b1..Synchronized

◆ CAN_ESR1_BOFFDONEINT [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_BOFFDONEINT ( x)

BOFFDONEINT - Bus Off Done Interrupt Flag 0b0..No such occurrence 0b1..FlexCAN module has completed Bus Off process.

◆ CAN_ESR1_ERRINT_FAST [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_ERRINT_FAST ( x)

ERRINT_FAST - Fast Error Interrupt Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..Error flag set in the data phase of CAN FD frames that have BRS = 1.

◆ CAN_ESR1_ERROVR [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_ERROVR ( x)

ERROVR - Error Overrun Flag 0b0..No overrun 0b1..Overrun

◆ CAN_ESR1_STFERR_FAST [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_STFERR_FAST ( x)

STFERR_FAST - Fast Stuffing Error Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..A stuffing error occurred since last read of this register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_FRMERR_FAST [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_FRMERR_FAST ( x)

FRMERR_FAST - Fast Form Error Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..A form error occurred since last read of this register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_CRCERR_FAST [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_CRCERR_FAST ( x)

CRCERR_FAST - Fast Cyclic Redundancy Check Error Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..A CRC error occurred since last read of this register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_BIT0ERR_FAST [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_BIT0ERR_FAST ( x)

BIT0ERR_FAST - Fast Bit0 Error Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..At least one bit transmitted as dominant is received as recessive.

◆ CAN_ESR1_BIT1ERR_FAST [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_BIT1ERR_FAST ( x)

BIT1ERR_FAST - Fast Bit1 Error Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..At least one bit transmitted as recessive is received as dominant.

◆ CAN_IMASK1_BUF31TO0M [1/3]

#define CAN_IMASK1_BUF31TO0M ( x)

BUF31TO0M - Buffer MBi Mask

◆ CAN_IFLAG1_BUF0I [1/3]

#define CAN_IFLAG1_BUF0I ( x)

BUF0I - Buffer MB0 Interrupt or Clear Legacy FIFO bit 0b0..MB0 has no occurrence of successfully completed transmission or reception. 0b1..MB0 has successfully completed transmission or reception.

◆ CAN_IFLAG1_BUF4TO1I [1/3]

#define CAN_IFLAG1_BUF4TO1I ( x)

BUF4TO1I - Buffer MBi Interrupt or Reserved

◆ CAN_IFLAG1_BUF5I [1/3]

#define CAN_IFLAG1_BUF5I ( x)

BUF5I - Buffer MB5 Interrupt or Frames available in Legacy RX FIFO 0b0..No occurrence of completed transmission or reception, or no frames available 0b1..MB5 completed transmission or reception, or frames available

◆ CAN_IFLAG1_BUF6I [1/3]

#define CAN_IFLAG1_BUF6I ( x)

BUF6I - Buffer MB6 Interrupt or Legacy RX FIFO Warning 0b0..No occurrence of MB6 completing transmission or reception, or FIFO not almost full. 0b1..MB6 completed transmission or reception, or FIFO almost full.

◆ CAN_IFLAG1_BUF7I [1/3]

#define CAN_IFLAG1_BUF7I ( x)

BUF7I - Buffer MB7 Interrupt or Legacy RX FIFO Overflow 0b0..No occurrence of MB7 completing transmission or reception, or no FIFO overflow. 0b1..MB7 completed transmission or reception, or FIFO overflow.

◆ CAN_IFLAG1_BUF31TO8I [1/3]

#define CAN_IFLAG1_BUF31TO8I ( x)

BUF31TO8I - Buffer MBi Interrupt

◆ CAN_CTRL2_PES [1/2]

#define CAN_CTRL2_PES ( x)

PES - Payload Byte and Bit Order Selection 0b0..Big-endian 0b1..Little-endian

◆ CAN_CTRL2_ASD [1/2]

#define CAN_CTRL2_ASD ( x)

ASD - ACK Suppression Disable 0b0..Enabled 0b1..Disabled

◆ CAN_CTRL2_EDFLTDIS [1/2]

#define CAN_CTRL2_EDFLTDIS ( x)

EDFLTDIS - Edge Filter Disable 0b0..Enabled 0b1..Disabled

◆ CAN_CTRL2_ISOCANFDEN [1/2]

#define CAN_CTRL2_ISOCANFDEN ( x)

ISOCANFDEN - ISO CAN FD Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_CTRL2_BTE [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_BTE ( x)

BTE - Bit Timing Expansion Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_CTRL2_PREXCEN [1/2]

#define CAN_CTRL2_PREXCEN ( x)

PREXCEN - Protocol Exception Enable 0b0..Disabled 0b1..Enabled

◆ CAN_CTRL2_EACEN [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_EACEN ( x)

EACEN - Entire Frame Arbitration Field Comparison Enable for RX Message Buffers 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_CTRL2_RRS [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_RRS ( x)

RRS - Remote Request Storing 0b0..Generated 0b1..Stored

◆ CAN_CTRL2_MRP [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_MRP ( x)

MRP - Message Buffers Reception Priority 0b0..Matching starts from Legacy RX FIFO or Enhanced RX FIFO and continues on message buffers. 0b1..Matching starts from message buffers and continues on Legacy RX FIFO or Enhanced RX FIFO.

◆ CAN_CTRL2_TASD [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_TASD ( x)

TASD - Transmission Arbitration Start Delay

◆ CAN_CTRL2_RFFN [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_RFFN ( x)

RFFN - Number of Legacy Receive FIFO Filters

◆ CAN_CTRL2_BOFFDONEMSK [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_BOFFDONEMSK ( x)

BOFFDONEMSK - Bus Off Done Interrupt Mask 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_CTRL2_ERRMSK_FAST [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_ERRMSK_FAST ( x)

ERRMSK_FAST - Error Interrupt Mask for Errors Detected in the Data Phase of Fast CAN FD Frames 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_ESR2_IMB [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR2_IMB ( x)

IMB - Inactive Message Buffer 0b0..Message buffer indicated by ESR2[LPTM] is not inactive. 0b1..At least one message buffer is inactive.

◆ CAN_ESR2_VPS [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR2_VPS ( x)

VPS - Valid Priority Status 0b0..Invalid 0b1..Valid

◆ CAN_ESR2_LPTM [1/3]

#define CAN_ESR2_LPTM ( x)

LPTM - Lowest Priority TX Message Buffer

◆ CAN_CRCR_TXCRC [1/3]

#define CAN_CRCR_TXCRC ( x)

TXCRC - Transmitted CRC value

◆ CAN_CRCR_MBCRC [1/3]

#define CAN_CRCR_MBCRC ( x)

MBCRC - CRC Message Buffer

◆ CAN_RXFGMASK_FGM [1/3]

#define CAN_RXFGMASK_FGM ( x)

FGM - Legacy RX FIFO Global Mask Bits

◆ CAN_RXFIR_IDHIT [1/3]

#define CAN_RXFIR_IDHIT ( x)

IDHIT - Identifier Acceptance Filter Hit Indicator

◆ CAN_CBT_EPSEG2 [1/3]

#define CAN_CBT_EPSEG2 ( x)

EPSEG2 - Extended Phase Segment 2

◆ CAN_CBT_EPSEG1 [1/3]

#define CAN_CBT_EPSEG1 ( x)

EPSEG1 - Extended Phase Segment 1

◆ CAN_CBT_EPROPSEG [1/3]

#define CAN_CBT_EPROPSEG ( x)

EPROPSEG - Extended Propagation Segment

◆ CAN_CBT_ERJW [1/3]

#define CAN_CBT_ERJW ( x)

ERJW - Extended Resync Jump Width

◆ CAN_CBT_EPRESDIV [1/3]

#define CAN_CBT_EPRESDIV ( x)

EPRESDIV - Extended Prescaler Division Factor

◆ CAN_CBT_BTF [1/3]

#define CAN_CBT_BTF ( x)

BTF - Bit Timing Format Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_CS_TIME_STAMP [1/3]

#define CAN_CS_TIME_STAMP ( x)

TIME_STAMP - Free-Running Counter Time stamp. This 16-bit field is a copy of the Free-Running Timer, captured for Tx and Rx frames at the time when the beginning of the Identifier field appears on the CAN bus.

◆ CAN_CS_DLC [1/3]

#define CAN_CS_DLC ( x)

DLC - Length of the data to be stored/transmitted.

◆ CAN_CS_RTR [1/3]

#define CAN_CS_RTR ( x)

RTR - Remote Transmission Request. One/zero for remote/data frame.

◆ CAN_CS_IDE [1/3]

#define CAN_CS_IDE ( x)

IDE - ID Extended. One/zero for extended/standard format frame.

◆ CAN_CS_SRR [1/3]

#define CAN_CS_SRR ( x)

SRR - Substitute Remote Request. Contains a fixed recessive bit.

◆ CAN_CS_CODE [1/3]

#define CAN_CS_CODE ( x)

CODE - Message Buffer Code. This 4-bit field can be accessed (read or write) by the CPU and by the FlexCAN module itself, as part of the message buffer matching and arbitration process.

◆ CAN_CS_ESI [1/3]

#define CAN_CS_ESI ( x)

ESI - Error State Indicator. This bit indicates if the transmitting node is error active or error passive.

◆ CAN_CS_BRS [1/3]

#define CAN_CS_BRS ( x)

BRS - Bit Rate Switch. This bit defines whether the bit rate is switched inside a CAN FD format frame.

◆ CAN_CS_EDL [1/3]

#define CAN_CS_EDL ( x)

EDL - Extended Data Length. This bit distinguishes between CAN format and CAN FD format frames. The EDL bit must not be set for Message Buffers configured to RANSWER with code field 0b1010.

◆ CAN_ID_EXT [1/3]

#define CAN_ID_EXT ( x)

EXT - Contains extended (LOW word) identifier of message buffer.

◆ CAN_ID_STD [1/3]

#define CAN_ID_STD ( x)

STD - Contains standard/extended (HIGH word) identifier of message buffer.

◆ CAN_ID_PRIO [1/3]

#define CAN_ID_PRIO ( x)

PRIO - Local priority. This 3-bit fieldis only used when LPRIO_EN bit is set in MCR and it only makes sense for Tx buffers. These bits are not transmitted. They are appended to the regular ID to define the transmission priority.

DATA_BYTE_3 - Data byte 0 of Rx/Tx frame.

◆ CAN_RXIMR_MI [1/3]

#define CAN_RXIMR_MI ( x)

MI - Individual Mask Bits

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PN_FCS [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PN_FCS ( x)

FCS - Filtering Combination Selection 0b00..Message ID filtering only 0b01..Message ID filtering and payload filtering 0b10..Message ID filtering occurring a specified number of times 0b11..Message ID filtering and payload filtering a specified number of times

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PN_IDFS [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PN_IDFS ( x)

IDFS - ID Filtering Selection 0b00..Match ID contents to an exact target value 0b01..Match an ID value greater than or equal to a specified target value 0b10..Match an ID value smaller than or equal to a specified target value 0b11..Match an ID value within a range of values, inclusive

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PN_PLFS [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PN_PLFS ( x)

PLFS - Payload Filtering Selection 0b00..Match payload contents to an exact target value 0b01..Match a payload value greater than or equal to a specified target value 0b10..Match a payload value smaller than or equal to a specified target value 0b11..Match upon a payload value within a range of values, inclusive

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PN_NMATCH [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PN_NMATCH ( x)

NMATCH - Number of Messages Matching the Same Filtering Criteria 0b00000001..Once 0b00000010..Twice 0b11111111..255 times

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PN_WUMF_MSK [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PN_WUMF_MSK ( x)

WUMF_MSK - Wake-up by Matching Flag Mask 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PN_WTOF_MSK [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PN_WTOF_MSK ( x)

WTOF_MSK - Wake-up by Timeout Flag Mask 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_CTRL2_PN_MATCHTO [1/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_PN_MATCHTO ( x)

MATCHTO - Timeout for No Message Matching the Filtering Criteria

◆ CAN_WU_MTC_MCOUNTER [1/3]

#define CAN_WU_MTC_MCOUNTER ( x)

MCOUNTER - Number of Matches in Pretended Networking

◆ CAN_WU_MTC_WUMF [1/3]

#define CAN_WU_MTC_WUMF ( x)

WUMF - Wake-up by Match Flag 0b0..No event detected 0b1..Event detected

◆ CAN_WU_MTC_WTOF [1/3]

#define CAN_WU_MTC_WTOF ( x)

WTOF - Wake-up by Timeout Flag Bit 0b0..No event detected 0b1..Event detected

◆ CAN_FLT_ID1_FLT_ID1 [1/3]

#define CAN_FLT_ID1_FLT_ID1 ( x)

FLT_ID1 - ID Filter 1 for Pretended Networking filtering

◆ CAN_FLT_ID1_FLT_RTR [1/3]

#define CAN_FLT_ID1_FLT_RTR ( x)

FLT_RTR - Remote Transmission Request Filter 0b0..Reject remote frame (accept data frame) 0b1..Accept remote frame

◆ CAN_FLT_ID1_FLT_IDE [1/3]

#define CAN_FLT_ID1_FLT_IDE ( x)

FLT_IDE - ID Extended Filter 0b0..Standard 0b1..Extended

◆ CAN_FLT_DLC_FLT_DLC_HI [1/3]

DMALW - DMA Last Word

◆ CAN_ERFCR_ERFEN [1/3]

ERFEN - Enhanced RX FIFO enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_ERFIER_ERFDAIE [1/3]

#define CAN_ERFIER_ERFDAIE ( x)

ERFDAIE - Enhanced RX FIFO Data Available Interrupt Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

#define CAN_ERFIER_ERFWMIIE ( x)

ERFWMIIE - Enhanced RX FIFO Watermark Indication Interrupt Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_ERFIER_ERFOVFIE [1/3]

ERFOVFIE - Enhanced RX FIFO Overflow Interrupt Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

ERFUFWIE - Enhanced RX FIFO Underflow Interrupt Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

ERFEL - Enhanced RX FIFO Elements

◆ CAN_ERFSR_ERFF [1/3]

#define CAN_ERFSR_ERFF ( x)

ERFF - Enhanced RX FIFO Full Flag 0b0..Not full 0b1..Full

◆ CAN_ERFSR_ERFE [1/3]

#define CAN_ERFSR_ERFE ( x)

ERFE - Enhanced RX FIFO Empty Flag 0b0..Not empty 0b1..Empty

◆ CAN_ERFSR_ERFCLR [1/3]

#define CAN_ERFSR_ERFCLR ( x)

ERFCLR - Enhanced RX FIFO Clear 0b0..No effect 0b1..Clear enhanced RX FIFO content

◆ CAN_ERFSR_ERFDA [1/3]

#define CAN_ERFSR_ERFDA ( x)

ERFDA - Enhanced RX FIFO Data Available Flag 0b0..No such occurrence 0b1..At least one message stored in Enhanced RX FIFO

◆ CAN_ERFSR_ERFWMI [1/3]

#define CAN_ERFSR_ERFWMI ( x)

ERFWMI - Enhanced RX FIFO Watermark Indication Flag 0b0..No such occurrence 0b1..Number of messages in FIFO is greater than the watermark

◆ CAN_ERFSR_ERFOVF [1/3]

#define CAN_ERFSR_ERFOVF ( x)

ERFOVF - Enhanced RX FIFO Overflow Flag 0b0..No such occurrence 0b1..Overflow

◆ CAN_ERFSR_ERFUFW [1/3]

#define CAN_ERFSR_ERFUFW ( x)

ERFUFW - Enhanced RX FIFO Underflow Flag 0b0..No such occurrence 0b1..Underflow

◆ CAN_ERFFEL_FEL [1/3]

#define CAN_ERFFEL_FEL ( x)

FEL - Filter Element Bits

◆ CAN_MCR_MAXMB [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_MAXMB ( x)

MAXMB - Number of the Last Message Buffer

◆ CAN_MCR_TPOE

#define CAN_MCR_TPOE ( x)

TPOE - TX Pin Override Enable 0b0..TX pin forcing is disabled 0b1..TX pin forcing is enabled

◆ CAN_MCR_IDAM [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_IDAM ( x)

IDAM - ID Acceptance Mode 0b00..Format A: One full ID (standard and extended) per ID filter table element. 0b01..Format B: Two full standard IDs or two partial 14-bit (standard and extended) IDs per ID filter table element. 0b10..Format C: Four partial 8-bit standard IDs per ID filter table element. 0b11..Format D: All frames rejected.

◆ CAN_MCR_TPOV

#define CAN_MCR_TPOV ( x)

TPOV - TX Pin Override Value 0b0..TX pin is forced to be dominant 0b1..TX pin is forced to be recessive

◆ CAN_MCR_FDEN [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_FDEN ( x)

FDEN - CAN FD Operation Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_AEN [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_AEN ( x)

AEN - Abort Enable 0b0..Disabled 0b1..Enabled

◆ CAN_MCR_LPRIOEN [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_LPRIOEN ( x)

LPRIOEN - Local Priority Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_PNET_EN [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_PNET_EN ( x)

PNET_EN - Pretended Networking Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_DMA [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_DMA ( x)

DMA - DMA Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_IRMQ [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_IRMQ ( x)

IRMQ - Individual RX Masking and Queue Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_SRXDIS [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_SRXDIS ( x)

SRXDIS - Self-Reception Disable 0b0..Enable 0b1..Disable

◆ CAN_MCR_DOZE [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_DOZE ( x)

DOZE - Doze Mode Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_WAKSRC [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_WAKSRC ( x)

WAKSRC - Wake-Up Source 0b0..No filter applied 0b1..Filter applied

◆ CAN_MCR_LPMACK [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_LPMACK ( x)

LPMACK - Low-Power Mode Acknowledge 0b0..Not in a low-power mode 0b1..In a low-power mode

◆ CAN_MCR_WRNEN [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_WRNEN ( x)

WRNEN - Warning Interrupt Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_SLFWAK [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_SLFWAK ( x)

SLFWAK - Self-Wake-Up Feature 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_SUPV [2/2]

#define CAN_MCR_SUPV ( x)

SUPV - Supervisor Mode 0b0..User mode 0b1..Supervisor mode

◆ CAN_MCR_FRZACK [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_FRZACK ( x)

FRZACK - Freeze Mode Acknowledge 0b0..Not in Freeze mode, prescaler running. 0b1..In Freeze mode, prescaler stopped.

◆ CAN_MCR_SOFTRST [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_SOFTRST ( x)

SOFTRST - Soft Reset 0b0..No reset 0b1..Soft reset affects reset registers

◆ CAN_MCR_WAKMSK [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_WAKMSK ( x)

WAKMSK - Wake-up Interrupt Mask 0b0..Disabled 0b1..Enabled

◆ CAN_MCR_NOTRDY [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_NOTRDY ( x)

NOTRDY - FlexCAN Not Ready 0b0..FlexCAN is in Normal mode, Listen-Only mode, or Loopback mode. 0b1..FlexCAN is in Disable mode, Doze mode, Stop mode, or Freeze mode.

◆ CAN_MCR_HALT [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_HALT ( x)

HALT - Halt FlexCAN 0b0..No request 0b1..Enter Freeze mode, if MCR[FRZ] = 1.

◆ CAN_MCR_RFEN [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_RFEN ( x)

RFEN - Legacy RX FIFO Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_FRZ [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_FRZ ( x)

FRZ - Freeze Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_MDIS [2/3]

#define CAN_MCR_MDIS ( x)

MDIS - Module Disable 0b0..Enable 0b1..Disable

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PROPSEG [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PROPSEG ( x)

PROPSEG - Propagation Segment

◆ CAN_CTRL1_LOM [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_LOM ( x)

LOM - Listen-Only Mode 0b0..Listen-Only mode is deactivated. 0b1..FlexCAN module operates in Listen-Only mode.

◆ CAN_CTRL1_LBUF [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_LBUF ( x)

LBUF - Lowest Buffer Transmitted First 0b0..Buffer with highest priority is transmitted first. 0b1..Lowest number buffer is transmitted first.

◆ CAN_CTRL1_TSYN [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_TSYN ( x)

TSYN - Timer Sync 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_CTRL1_BOFFREC [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_BOFFREC ( x)

BOFFREC - Bus Off Recovery 0b0..Enabled 0b1..Disabled

◆ CAN_CTRL1_SMP [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_SMP ( x)

SMP - CAN Bit Sampling 0b0..One sample is used to determine the bit value. 0b1..Three samples are used to determine the value of the received bit: the regular one (sample point) and two preceding samples. A majority rule is used.

◆ CAN_CTRL1_ROM

#define CAN_CTRL1_ROM ( x)

ROM - Restricted Operation Mode 0b0..Restricted operation is disabled 0b1..Restricted operation is enabled

◆ CAN_CTRL1_RWRNMSK [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_RWRNMSK ( x)

RWRNMSK - RX Warning Interrupt Mask 0b0..Disabled 0b1..Enabled

◆ CAN_CTRL1_TWRNMSK [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_TWRNMSK ( x)

TWRNMSK - TX Warning Interrupt Mask 0b0..Disabled 0b1..Enabled

◆ CAN_CTRL1_LPB [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_LPB ( x)

LPB - Loopback Mode 0b0..Disabled 0b1..Enabled

◆ CAN_CTRL1_ERRMSK [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_ERRMSK ( x)

ERRMSK - Error Interrupt Mask 0b0..Interrupt disabled 0b1..Interrupt enabled

◆ CAN_CTRL1_BOFFMSK [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_BOFFMSK ( x)

BOFFMSK - Bus Off Interrupt Mask 0b0..Interrupt disabled 0b1..Interrupt enabled

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PSEG2 [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PSEG2 ( x)

PSEG2 - Phase Segment 2

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PSEG1 [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PSEG1 ( x)

PSEG1 - Phase Segment 1

◆ CAN_CTRL1_RJW [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_RJW ( x)

RJW - Resync Jump Width

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PRESDIV [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PRESDIV ( x)

PRESDIV - Prescaler Division Factor

◆ CAN_TIMER_TIMER [2/3]

#define CAN_TIMER_TIMER ( x)

TIMER - Timer Value

◆ CAN_TCR_DSCACK [1/2]

#define CAN_TCR_DSCACK ( x)

DSCACK - Discard Acknowledge 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..Discard acknowledge bit.

◆ CAN_TCR_BIT_CLS [1/2]

#define CAN_TCR_BIT_CLS ( x)

BIT_CLS - Output Bit Clock 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..Internal FlexCAN bit clock on ipp_do_cantx pin.

◆ CAN_TCR_TRD [1/2]

#define CAN_TCR_TRD ( x)

TRD - Test Read 0b0..Read operations behave as in Normal mode. 0b1..Read access by CPU does not cause changes in registers or state variables.

◆ CAN_RXMGMASK_MG [2/3]

#define CAN_RXMGMASK_MG ( x)

MG - Global Mask for RX Message Buffers

◆ CAN_RX14MASK_RX14M [2/3]

#define CAN_RX14MASK_RX14M ( x)

RX14M - RX Buffer 14 Mask Bits

◆ CAN_RX15MASK_RX15M [2/3]

#define CAN_RX15MASK_RX15M ( x)

RX15M - RX Buffer 15 Mask Bits

◆ CAN_ECR_TXERRCNT [2/3]

#define CAN_ECR_TXERRCNT ( x)

TXERRCNT - Transmit Error Counter

◆ CAN_ECR_RXERRCNT [2/3]

#define CAN_ECR_RXERRCNT ( x)

RXERRCNT - Receive Error Counter

◆ CAN_ECR_TXERRCNT_FAST [2/3]

#define CAN_ECR_TXERRCNT_FAST ( x)

TXERRCNT_FAST - Transmit Error Counter for Fast Bits

◆ CAN_ECR_RXERRCNT_FAST [2/3]

#define CAN_ECR_RXERRCNT_FAST ( x)

RXERRCNT_FAST - Receive Error Counter for Fast Bits

◆ CAN_ESR1_WAKINT [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_WAKINT ( x)

WAKINT - Wake-up Interrupt Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..Indicates that a recessive-to-dominant transition was received on the CAN bus.

◆ CAN_ESR1_ERRINT [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_ERRINT ( x)

ERRINT - Error Interrupt Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..Indicates setting of any error flag in the Error and Status register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_BOFFINT [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_BOFFINT ( x)

BOFFINT - Bus Off Interrupt Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..FlexCAN module entered Bus Off state.

◆ CAN_ESR1_RX [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_RX ( x)

RX - FlexCAN in Reception Flag 0b0..Not receiving 0b1..Receiving

◆ CAN_ESR1_FLTCONF [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_FLTCONF ( x)

FLTCONF - Fault Confinement State 0b00..Error Active 0b01..Error Passive 0b1x..Bus Off

◆ CAN_ESR1_TX [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_TX ( x)

TX - FlexCAN In Transmission 0b0..Not transmitting 0b1..Transmitting

◆ CAN_ESR1_IDLE [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_IDLE ( x)

IDLE - Idle 0b0..Not IDLE 0b1..IDLE

◆ CAN_ESR1_RXWRN [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_RXWRN ( x)

RXWRN - RX Error Warning Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..RXERRCNT is greater than or equal to 96.

◆ CAN_ESR1_TXWRN [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_TXWRN ( x)

TXWRN - TX Error Warning Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..TXERRCNT is 96 or greater.

◆ CAN_ESR1_STFERR [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_STFERR ( x)

STFERR - Stuffing Error Flag 0b0..No error 0b1..Error occurred since last read of this register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_FRMERR [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_FRMERR ( x)

FRMERR - Form Error Flag 0b0..No error 0b1..Error occurred since last read of this register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_CRCERR [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_CRCERR ( x)

CRCERR - Cyclic Redundancy Check Error Flag 0b0..No error 0b1..Error occurred since last read of this register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_ACKERR [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_ACKERR ( x)

ACKERR - Acknowledge Error Flag 0b0..No error 0b1..Error occurred since last read of this register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_BIT0ERR [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_BIT0ERR ( x)

BIT0ERR - Bit0 Error Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..At least one bit sent as dominant is received as recessive.

◆ CAN_ESR1_BIT1ERR [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_BIT1ERR ( x)

BIT1ERR - Bit1 Error Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..At least one bit sent as recessive is received as dominant.

◆ CAN_ESR1_RWRNINT [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_RWRNINT ( x)

RWRNINT - RX Warning Interrupt Flag 0b0..No such occurrence 0b1..RX error counter changed from less than 96 to greater than or equal to 96.

◆ CAN_ESR1_TWRNINT [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_TWRNINT ( x)

TWRNINT - TX Warning Interrupt Flag 0b0..No such occurrence 0b1..TX error counter changed from less than 96 to greater than or equal to 96.

◆ CAN_ESR1_SYNCH [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_SYNCH ( x)

SYNCH - CAN Synchronization Status Flag 0b0..Not synchronized 0b1..Synchronized

◆ CAN_ESR1_BOFFDONEINT [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_BOFFDONEINT ( x)

BOFFDONEINT - Bus Off Done Interrupt Flag 0b0..No such occurrence 0b1..FlexCAN module has completed Bus Off process.

◆ CAN_ESR1_ERRINT_FAST [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_ERRINT_FAST ( x)

ERRINT_FAST - Fast Error Interrupt Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..Error flag set in the data phase of CAN FD frames that have BRS = 1.

◆ CAN_ESR1_ERROVR [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_ERROVR ( x)

ERROVR - Error Overrun Flag 0b0..No overrun 0b1..Overrun

◆ CAN_ESR1_ATP

#define CAN_ESR1_ATP ( x)

ATP - Active to Passive State 0b0..Does not transition 0b1..Transitions

◆ CAN_ESR1_PTA

#define CAN_ESR1_PTA ( x)

PTA - Passive to Active State 0b0..Does not transition 0b1..Transitions

◆ CAN_ESR1_STFERR_FAST [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_STFERR_FAST ( x)

STFERR_FAST - Fast Stuffing Error Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..A stuffing error occurred since last read of this register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_FRMERR_FAST [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_FRMERR_FAST ( x)

FRMERR_FAST - Fast Form Error Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..A form error occurred since last read of this register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_CRCERR_FAST [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_CRCERR_FAST ( x)

CRCERR_FAST - Fast Cyclic Redundancy Check Error Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..A CRC error occurred since last read of this register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_BIT0ERR_FAST [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_BIT0ERR_FAST ( x)

BIT0ERR_FAST - Fast Bit0 Error Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..At least one bit transmitted as dominant is received as recessive.

◆ CAN_ESR1_BIT1ERR_FAST [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_BIT1ERR_FAST ( x)

BIT1ERR_FAST - Fast Bit1 Error Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..At least one bit transmitted as recessive is received as dominant.

◆ CAN_IMASK1_BUF31TO0M [2/3]

#define CAN_IMASK1_BUF31TO0M ( x)

BUF31TO0M - Buffer MBi Mask

◆ CAN_IFLAG1_BUF0I [2/3]

#define CAN_IFLAG1_BUF0I ( x)

BUF0I - Buffer MB0 Interrupt or Clear Legacy FIFO bit 0b0..MB0 has no occurrence of successfully completed transmission or reception. 0b1..MB0 has successfully completed transmission or reception.

◆ CAN_IFLAG1_BUF4TO1I [2/3]

#define CAN_IFLAG1_BUF4TO1I ( x)

BUF4TO1I - Buffer MBi Interrupt or Reserved

◆ CAN_IFLAG1_BUF5I [2/3]

#define CAN_IFLAG1_BUF5I ( x)

BUF5I - Buffer MB5 Interrupt or Frames available in Legacy RX FIFO 0b0..No occurrence of completed transmission or reception, or no frames available 0b1..MB5 completed transmission or reception, or frames available

◆ CAN_IFLAG1_BUF6I [2/3]

#define CAN_IFLAG1_BUF6I ( x)

BUF6I - Buffer MB6 Interrupt or Legacy RX FIFO Warning 0b0..No occurrence of MB6 completing transmission or reception, or FIFO not almost full. 0b1..MB6 completed transmission or reception, or FIFO almost full.

◆ CAN_IFLAG1_BUF7I [2/3]

#define CAN_IFLAG1_BUF7I ( x)

BUF7I - Buffer MB7 Interrupt or Legacy RX FIFO Overflow 0b0..No occurrence of MB7 completing transmission or reception, or no FIFO overflow. 0b1..MB7 completed transmission or reception, or FIFO overflow.

◆ CAN_IFLAG1_BUF31TO8I [2/3]

#define CAN_IFLAG1_BUF31TO8I ( x)

BUF31TO8I - Buffer MBi Interrupt

◆ CAN_CTRL2_RETRY

#define CAN_CTRL2_RETRY ( x)

RETRY - Number of Retransmission Requests 0b000..No retransmission 0b001..Count of re-transmission attempts 0b010..Count of re-transmission attempts 0b011..Count of re-transmission attempts 0b100..Count of re-transmission attempts 0b101..Count of re-transmission attempts 0b110..Count of re-transmission attempts 0b111..Unlimited number of retransmission

◆ CAN_CTRL2_FLT_RXN

#define CAN_CTRL2_FLT_RXN ( x)

FLT_RXN - Fault reaction 0b0..Fault reaction is disabled 0b1..Fault reaction is enabled

◆ CAN_CTRL2_BTE [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_BTE ( x)

BTE - Bit Timing Expansion Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_CTRL2_EACEN [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_EACEN ( x)

EACEN - Entire Frame Arbitration Field Comparison Enable for RX Message Buffers 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_CTRL2_RRS [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_RRS ( x)

RRS - Remote Request Storing 0b0..Generated 0b1..Stored

◆ CAN_CTRL2_MRP [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_MRP ( x)

MRP - Message Buffers Reception Priority 0b0..Matching starts from Legacy RX FIFO or Enhanced RX FIFO and continues on message buffers. 0b1..Matching starts from message buffers and continues on Legacy RX FIFO or Enhanced RX FIFO.

◆ CAN_CTRL2_TASD [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_TASD ( x)

TASD - Transmission Arbitration Start Delay

◆ CAN_CTRL2_RFFN [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_RFFN ( x)

RFFN - Number of Legacy Receive FIFO Filters

◆ CAN_CTRL2_BOFFDONEMSK [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_BOFFDONEMSK ( x)

BOFFDONEMSK - Bus Off Done Interrupt Mask 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_CTRL2_ERRMSK_FAST [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_ERRMSK_FAST ( x)

ERRMSK_FAST - Error Interrupt Mask for Errors Detected in the Data Phase of Fast CAN FD Frames 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_ESR2_RX_PIN_ST

#define CAN_ESR2_RX_PIN_ST ( x)

RX_PIN_ST - RX Pin Status 0b0..RX pin is in the dominant state 0b1..RX pin is in a recessive state

◆ CAN_ESR2_IMB [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR2_IMB ( x)

IMB - Inactive Message Buffer 0b0..Message buffer indicated by ESR2[LPTM] is not inactive. 0b1..At least one message buffer is inactive.

◆ CAN_ESR2_VPS [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR2_VPS ( x)

VPS - Valid Priority Status 0b0..Invalid 0b1..Valid

◆ CAN_ESR2_LPTM [2/3]

#define CAN_ESR2_LPTM ( x)

LPTM - Lowest Priority TX Message Buffer

◆ CAN_CRCR_TXCRC [2/3]

#define CAN_CRCR_TXCRC ( x)

TXCRC - Transmitted CRC value

◆ CAN_CRCR_MBCRC [2/3]

#define CAN_CRCR_MBCRC ( x)

MBCRC - CRC Message Buffer

◆ CAN_RXFGMASK_FGM [2/3]

#define CAN_RXFGMASK_FGM ( x)

FGM - Legacy RX FIFO Global Mask Bits

◆ CAN_RXFIR_IDHIT [2/3]

#define CAN_RXFIR_IDHIT ( x)

IDHIT - Identifier Acceptance Filter Hit Indicator

◆ CAN_CBT_EPSEG2 [2/3]

#define CAN_CBT_EPSEG2 ( x)

EPSEG2 - Extended Phase Segment 2

◆ CAN_CBT_EPSEG1 [2/3]

#define CAN_CBT_EPSEG1 ( x)

EPSEG1 - Extended Phase Segment 1

◆ CAN_CBT_EPROPSEG [2/3]

#define CAN_CBT_EPROPSEG ( x)

EPROPSEG - Extended Propagation Segment

◆ CAN_CBT_ERJW [2/3]

#define CAN_CBT_ERJW ( x)

ERJW - Extended Resync Jump Width

◆ CAN_CBT_EPRESDIV [2/3]

#define CAN_CBT_EPRESDIV ( x)

EPRESDIV - Extended Prescaler Division Factor

◆ CAN_CBT_BTF [2/3]

#define CAN_CBT_BTF ( x)

BTF - Bit Timing Format Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_DBG1_CFSM [1/2]

#define CAN_DBG1_CFSM ( x)

CFSM - CAN Finite State Machine

◆ CAN_DBG1_CBN [1/2]

#define CAN_DBG1_CBN ( x)

CBN - CAN Bit Number

◆ CAN_DBG2_RMP [1/2]

#define CAN_DBG2_RMP ( x)

RMP - RX Matching Pointer

◆ CAN_DBG2_MPP [1/2]

#define CAN_DBG2_MPP ( x)

MPP - Matching Process in Progress 0b0..No matching process ongoing. 0b1..Matching process is in progress.

◆ CAN_DBG2_TAP [1/2]

#define CAN_DBG2_TAP ( x)

TAP - TX Arbitration Pointer

◆ CAN_DBG2_APP [1/2]

#define CAN_DBG2_APP ( x)

APP - Arbitration Process in Progress 0b0..No arbitration process ongoing. 0b1..Arbitration process is in progress.

◆ CAN_ET_TIMER

#define CAN_ET_TIMER ( x)

TIMER - Timer

◆ CAN_FLTCONF_IE_ATP_IE

#define CAN_FLTCONF_IE_ATP_IE ( x)

ATP_IE - Active to Passive Interrupt Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_FLTCONF_IE_PTA_IE

#define CAN_FLTCONF_IE_PTA_IE ( x)

PTA_IE - Passive to Active Interrupt Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_CS_TIME_STAMP [2/3]

#define CAN_CS_TIME_STAMP ( x)

TIME_STAMP - Free-Running Counter Time stamp. This 16-bit field is a copy of the Free-Running Timer, captured for Tx and Rx frames at the time when the beginning of the Identifier field appears on the CAN bus.

◆ CAN_CS_DLC [2/3]

#define CAN_CS_DLC ( x)

DLC - Length of the data to be stored/transmitted.

◆ CAN_CS_RTR [2/3]

#define CAN_CS_RTR ( x)

RTR - Remote Transmission Request. One/zero for remote/data frame.

◆ CAN_CS_IDE [2/3]

#define CAN_CS_IDE ( x)

IDE - ID Extended. One/zero for extended/standard format frame.

◆ CAN_CS_SRR [2/3]

#define CAN_CS_SRR ( x)

SRR - Substitute Remote Request. Contains a fixed recessive bit.

◆ CAN_CS_CODE [2/3]

#define CAN_CS_CODE ( x)

CODE - Message Buffer Code. This 4-bit field can be accessed (read or write) by the CPU and by the FlexCAN module itself, as part of the message buffer matching and arbitration process.

◆ CAN_CS_ESI [2/3]

#define CAN_CS_ESI ( x)

ESI - Error State Indicator. This bit indicates if the transmitting node is error active or error passive.

◆ CAN_CS_BRS [2/3]

#define CAN_CS_BRS ( x)

BRS - Bit Rate Switch. This bit defines whether the bit rate is switched inside a CAN FD format frame.

◆ CAN_CS_EDL [2/3]

#define CAN_CS_EDL ( x)

EDL - Extended Data Length. This bit distinguishes between CAN format and CAN FD format frames. The EDL bit must not be set for Message Buffers configured to RANSWER with code field 0b1010.

◆ CAN_ID_EXT [2/3]

#define CAN_ID_EXT ( x)

EXT - Contains extended (LOW word) identifier of message buffer.

◆ CAN_ID_STD [2/3]

#define CAN_ID_STD ( x)

STD - Contains standard/extended (HIGH word) identifier of message buffer.

◆ CAN_ID_PRIO [2/3]

#define CAN_ID_PRIO ( x)

PRIO - Local priority. This 3-bit fieldis only used when LPRIO_EN bit is set in MCR and it only makes sense for Tx buffers. These bits are not transmitted. They are appended to the regular ID to define the transmission priority.

DATA_BYTE_3 - Data byte 0 of Rx/Tx frame.

◆ CAN_RXIMR_MI [2/3]

#define CAN_RXIMR_MI ( x)

MI - Individual Mask Bits

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PN_FCS [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PN_FCS ( x)

FCS - Filtering Combination Selection 0b00..Message ID filtering only 0b01..Message ID filtering and payload filtering 0b10..Message ID filtering occurring a specified number of times 0b11..Message ID filtering and payload filtering a specified number of times

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PN_IDFS [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PN_IDFS ( x)

IDFS - ID Filtering Selection 0b00..Match ID contents to an exact target value 0b01..Match an ID value greater than or equal to a specified target value 0b10..Match an ID value smaller than or equal to a specified target value 0b11..Match an ID value within a range of values, inclusive

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PN_PLFS [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PN_PLFS ( x)

PLFS - Payload Filtering Selection 0b00..Match payload contents to an exact target value 0b01..Match a payload value greater than or equal to a specified target value 0b10..Match a payload value smaller than or equal to a specified target value 0b11..Match upon a payload value within a range of values, inclusive

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PN_NMATCH [2/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PN_NMATCH ( x)

NMATCH - Number of Messages Matching the Same Filtering Criteria 0b00000001..Once 0b00000010..Twice 0b11111111..255 times

#define CAN_CTRL1_PN_WUMF_MSK ( x)

ERFEL - Enhanced RX FIFO Elements

◆ CAN_ERFSR_ERFF [2/3]

ERFF - Enhanced RX FIFO Full Flag 0b0..Not full 0b1..Full

◆ CAN_ERFSR_ERFE [2/3]

ERFE - Enhanced RX FIFO Empty Flag 0b0..Not empty 0b1..Empty

◆ CAN_ERFSR_ERFCLR [2/3]

#define CAN_ERFSR_ERFCLR ( x)

ERFCLR - Enhanced RX FIFO Clear 0b0..No effect 0b1..Clear enhanced RX FIFO content

◆ CAN_ERFSR_ERFDA [2/3]

#define CAN_ERFSR_ERFDA ( x)

ERFDA - Enhanced RX FIFO Data Available Flag 0b0..No such occurrence 0b1..At least one message stored in Enhanced RX FIFO

◆ CAN_ERFSR_ERFWMI [2/3]

#define CAN_ERFSR_ERFWMI ( x)

ERFWMI - Enhanced RX FIFO Watermark Indication Flag 0b0..No such occurrence 0b1..Number of messages in FIFO is greater than the watermark

◆ CAN_ERFSR_ERFOVF [2/3]

#define CAN_ERFSR_ERFOVF ( x)

ERFOVF - Enhanced RX FIFO Overflow Flag 0b0..No such occurrence 0b1..Overflow

#define CAN_ERFSR_ERFUFW ( x)

ERFUFW - Enhanced RX FIFO Underflow Flag 0b0..No such occurrence 0b1..Underflow

#define CAN_ERFFEL_FEL ( x)

FEL - Filter Element Bits

◆ CAN_MCR_MAXMB [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_MAXMB ( x)

MAXMB - Number of the Last Message Buffer

◆ CAN_MCR_IDAM [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_IDAM ( x)

IDAM - ID Acceptance Mode 0b00..Format A: One full ID (standard and extended) per ID filter table element. 0b01..Format B: Two full standard IDs or two partial 14-bit (standard and extended) IDs per ID filter table element. 0b10..Format C: Four partial 8-bit standard IDs per ID filter table element. 0b11..Format D: All frames rejected.

◆ CAN_MCR_FDEN [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_FDEN ( x)

FDEN - CAN FD Operation Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_AEN [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_AEN ( x)

AEN - Abort Enable 0b0..Disabled 0b1..Enabled

◆ CAN_MCR_LPRIOEN [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_LPRIOEN ( x)

LPRIOEN - Local Priority Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_PNET_EN [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_PNET_EN ( x)

PNET_EN - Pretended Networking Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_DMA [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_DMA ( x)

DMA - DMA Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_IRMQ [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_IRMQ ( x)

IRMQ - Individual RX Masking and Queue Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_SRXDIS [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_SRXDIS ( x)

SRXDIS - Self-Reception Disable 0b0..Enable 0b1..Disable

◆ CAN_MCR_DOZE [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_DOZE ( x)

DOZE - Doze Mode Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_WAKSRC [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_WAKSRC ( x)

WAKSRC - Wake-Up Source 0b0..No filter applied 0b1..Filter applied

◆ CAN_MCR_LPMACK [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_LPMACK ( x)

LPMACK - Low-Power Mode Acknowledge 0b0..Not in a low-power mode 0b1..In a low-power mode

◆ CAN_MCR_WRNEN [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_WRNEN ( x)

WRNEN - Warning Interrupt Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_SLFWAK [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_SLFWAK ( x)

SLFWAK - Self-Wake-Up Feature 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_FRZACK [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_FRZACK ( x)

FRZACK - Freeze Mode Acknowledge 0b0..Not in Freeze mode, prescaler running. 0b1..In Freeze mode, prescaler stopped.

◆ CAN_MCR_SOFTRST [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_SOFTRST ( x)

SOFTRST - Soft Reset 0b0..No reset 0b1..Soft reset affects reset registers

◆ CAN_MCR_WAKMSK [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_WAKMSK ( x)

WAKMSK - Wake-up Interrupt Mask 0b0..Disabled 0b1..Enabled

◆ CAN_MCR_NOTRDY [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_NOTRDY ( x)

NOTRDY - FlexCAN Not Ready 0b0..FlexCAN is in Normal mode, Listen-Only mode, or Loopback mode. 0b1..FlexCAN is in Disable mode, Doze mode, Stop mode, or Freeze mode.

◆ CAN_MCR_HALT [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_HALT ( x)

HALT - Halt FlexCAN 0b0..No request 0b1..Enter Freeze mode, if MCR[FRZ] = 1.

◆ CAN_MCR_RFEN [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_RFEN ( x)

RFEN - Legacy RX FIFO Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_FRZ [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_FRZ ( x)

FRZ - Freeze Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_MCR_MDIS [3/3]

#define CAN_MCR_MDIS ( x)

MDIS - Module Disable 0b0..Enable 0b1..Disable

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PROPSEG [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PROPSEG ( x)

PROPSEG - Propagation Segment

◆ CAN_CTRL1_LOM [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_LOM ( x)

LOM - Listen-Only Mode 0b0..Listen-Only mode is deactivated. 0b1..FlexCAN module operates in Listen-Only mode.

◆ CAN_CTRL1_LBUF [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_LBUF ( x)

LBUF - Lowest Buffer Transmitted First 0b0..Buffer with highest priority is transmitted first. 0b1..Lowest number buffer is transmitted first.

◆ CAN_CTRL1_TSYN [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_TSYN ( x)

TSYN - Timer Sync 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_CTRL1_BOFFREC [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_BOFFREC ( x)

BOFFREC - Bus Off Recovery 0b0..Enabled 0b1..Disabled

◆ CAN_CTRL1_SMP [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_SMP ( x)

SMP - CAN Bit Sampling 0b0..One sample is used to determine the bit value. 0b1..Three samples are used to determine the value of the received bit: the regular one (sample point) and two preceding samples. A majority rule is used.

◆ CAN_CTRL1_RWRNMSK [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_RWRNMSK ( x)

RWRNMSK - RX Warning Interrupt Mask 0b0..Disabled 0b1..Enabled

◆ CAN_CTRL1_TWRNMSK [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_TWRNMSK ( x)

TWRNMSK - TX Warning Interrupt Mask 0b0..Disabled 0b1..Enabled

◆ CAN_CTRL1_LPB [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_LPB ( x)

LPB - Loopback Mode 0b0..Disabled 0b1..Enabled

◆ CAN_CTRL1_ERRMSK [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_ERRMSK ( x)

ERRMSK - Error Interrupt Mask 0b0..Interrupt disabled 0b1..Interrupt enabled

◆ CAN_CTRL1_BOFFMSK [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_BOFFMSK ( x)

BOFFMSK - Bus Off Interrupt Mask 0b0..Interrupt disabled 0b1..Interrupt enabled

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PSEG2 [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PSEG2 ( x)

PSEG2 - Phase Segment 2

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PSEG1 [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PSEG1 ( x)

PSEG1 - Phase Segment 1

◆ CAN_CTRL1_RJW [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_RJW ( x)

RJW - Resync Jump Width

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PRESDIV [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PRESDIV ( x)

PRESDIV - Prescaler Division Factor

◆ CAN_TIMER_TIMER [3/3]

#define CAN_TIMER_TIMER ( x)

TIMER - Timer Value

◆ CAN_TCR_DSCACK [2/2]

#define CAN_TCR_DSCACK ( x)

DSCACK - Discard Acknowledge 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..Discard acknowledge bit.

◆ CAN_TCR_BIT_CLS [2/2]

#define CAN_TCR_BIT_CLS ( x)

BIT_CLS - Output Bit Clock 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..Internal FlexCAN bit clock on ipp_do_cantx pin.

◆ CAN_TCR_TRD [2/2]

#define CAN_TCR_TRD ( x)

TRD - Test Read 0b0..Read operations behave as in Normal mode. 0b1..Read access by CPU does not cause changes in registers or state variables.

◆ CAN_RXMGMASK_MG [3/3]

#define CAN_RXMGMASK_MG ( x)

MG - Global Mask for RX Message Buffers

◆ CAN_RX14MASK_RX14M [3/3]

#define CAN_RX14MASK_RX14M ( x)

RX14M - RX Buffer 14 Mask Bits

◆ CAN_RX15MASK_RX15M [3/3]

#define CAN_RX15MASK_RX15M ( x)

RX15M - RX Buffer 15 Mask Bits

◆ CAN_ECR_TXERRCNT [3/3]

#define CAN_ECR_TXERRCNT ( x)

TXERRCNT - Transmit Error Counter

◆ CAN_ECR_RXERRCNT [3/3]

#define CAN_ECR_RXERRCNT ( x)

RXERRCNT - Receive Error Counter

◆ CAN_ECR_TXERRCNT_FAST [3/3]

#define CAN_ECR_TXERRCNT_FAST ( x)

TXERRCNT_FAST - Transmit Error Counter for Fast Bits

◆ CAN_ECR_RXERRCNT_FAST [3/3]

#define CAN_ECR_RXERRCNT_FAST ( x)

RXERRCNT_FAST - Receive Error Counter for Fast Bits

◆ CAN_ESR1_WAKINT [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_WAKINT ( x)

WAKINT - Wake-up Interrupt Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..Indicates that a recessive-to-dominant transition was received on the CAN bus.

◆ CAN_ESR1_ERRINT [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_ERRINT ( x)

ERRINT - Error Interrupt Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..Indicates setting of any error flag in the Error and Status register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_BOFFINT [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_BOFFINT ( x)

BOFFINT - Bus Off Interrupt Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..FlexCAN module entered Bus Off state.

◆ CAN_ESR1_RX [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_RX ( x)

RX - FlexCAN in Reception Flag 0b0..Not receiving 0b1..Receiving

◆ CAN_ESR1_FLTCONF [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_FLTCONF ( x)

FLTCONF - Fault Confinement State 0b00..Error Active 0b01..Error Passive 0b1x..Bus Off

◆ CAN_ESR1_TX [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_TX ( x)

TX - FlexCAN In Transmission 0b0..Not transmitting 0b1..Transmitting

◆ CAN_ESR1_IDLE [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_IDLE ( x)

IDLE - Idle 0b0..Not IDLE 0b1..IDLE

◆ CAN_ESR1_RXWRN [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_RXWRN ( x)

RXWRN - RX Error Warning Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..RXERRCNT is greater than or equal to 96.

◆ CAN_ESR1_TXWRN [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_TXWRN ( x)

TXWRN - TX Error Warning Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..TXERRCNT is 96 or greater.

◆ CAN_ESR1_STFERR [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_STFERR ( x)

STFERR - Stuffing Error Flag 0b0..No error 0b1..Error occurred since last read of this register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_FRMERR [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_FRMERR ( x)

FRMERR - Form Error Flag 0b0..No error 0b1..Error occurred since last read of this register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_CRCERR [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_CRCERR ( x)

CRCERR - Cyclic Redundancy Check Error Flag 0b0..No error 0b1..Error occurred since last read of this register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_ACKERR [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_ACKERR ( x)

ACKERR - Acknowledge Error Flag 0b0..No error 0b1..Error occurred since last read of this register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_BIT0ERR [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_BIT0ERR ( x)

BIT0ERR - Bit0 Error Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..At least one bit sent as dominant is received as recessive.

◆ CAN_ESR1_BIT1ERR [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_BIT1ERR ( x)

BIT1ERR - Bit1 Error Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..At least one bit sent as recessive is received as dominant.

◆ CAN_ESR1_RWRNINT [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_RWRNINT ( x)

RWRNINT - RX Warning Interrupt Flag 0b0..No such occurrence 0b1..RX error counter changed from less than 96 to greater than or equal to 96.

◆ CAN_ESR1_TWRNINT [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_TWRNINT ( x)

TWRNINT - TX Warning Interrupt Flag 0b0..No such occurrence 0b1..TX error counter changed from less than 96 to greater than or equal to 96.

◆ CAN_ESR1_SYNCH [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_SYNCH ( x)

SYNCH - CAN Synchronization Status Flag 0b0..Not synchronized 0b1..Synchronized

◆ CAN_ESR1_BOFFDONEINT [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_BOFFDONEINT ( x)

BOFFDONEINT - Bus Off Done Interrupt Flag 0b0..No such occurrence 0b1..FlexCAN module has completed Bus Off process.

◆ CAN_ESR1_ERRINT_FAST [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_ERRINT_FAST ( x)

ERRINT_FAST - Fast Error Interrupt Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..Error flag set in the data phase of CAN FD frames that have BRS = 1.

◆ CAN_ESR1_ERROVR [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_ERROVR ( x)

ERROVR - Error Overrun Flag 0b0..No overrun 0b1..Overrun

◆ CAN_ESR1_STFERR_FAST [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_STFERR_FAST ( x)

STFERR_FAST - Fast Stuffing Error Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..A stuffing error occurred since last read of this register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_FRMERR_FAST [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_FRMERR_FAST ( x)

FRMERR_FAST - Fast Form Error Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..A form error occurred since last read of this register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_CRCERR_FAST [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_CRCERR_FAST ( x)

CRCERR_FAST - Fast Cyclic Redundancy Check Error Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..A CRC error occurred since last read of this register.

◆ CAN_ESR1_BIT0ERR_FAST [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_BIT0ERR_FAST ( x)

BIT0ERR_FAST - Fast Bit0 Error Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..At least one bit transmitted as dominant is received as recessive.

◆ CAN_ESR1_BIT1ERR_FAST [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR1_BIT1ERR_FAST ( x)

BIT1ERR_FAST - Fast Bit1 Error Flag 0b0..No such occurrence. 0b1..At least one bit transmitted as recessive is received as dominant.

◆ CAN_IMASK1_BUF31TO0M [3/3]

#define CAN_IMASK1_BUF31TO0M ( x)

BUF31TO0M - Buffer MBi Mask

◆ CAN_IFLAG1_BUF0I [3/3]

#define CAN_IFLAG1_BUF0I ( x)

BUF0I - Buffer MB0 Interrupt or Clear Legacy FIFO bit 0b0..MB0 has no occurrence of successfully completed transmission or reception. 0b1..MB0 has successfully completed transmission or reception.

◆ CAN_IFLAG1_BUF4TO1I [3/3]

#define CAN_IFLAG1_BUF4TO1I ( x)

BUF4TO1I - Buffer MBi Interrupt or Reserved

◆ CAN_IFLAG1_BUF5I [3/3]

#define CAN_IFLAG1_BUF5I ( x)

BUF5I - Buffer MB5 Interrupt or Frames available in Legacy RX FIFO 0b0..No occurrence of completed transmission or reception, or no frames available 0b1..MB5 completed transmission or reception, or frames available

◆ CAN_IFLAG1_BUF6I [3/3]

#define CAN_IFLAG1_BUF6I ( x)

BUF6I - Buffer MB6 Interrupt or Legacy RX FIFO Warning 0b0..No occurrence of MB6 completing transmission or reception, or FIFO not almost full. 0b1..MB6 completed transmission or reception, or FIFO almost full.

◆ CAN_IFLAG1_BUF7I [3/3]

#define CAN_IFLAG1_BUF7I ( x)

BUF7I - Buffer MB7 Interrupt or Legacy RX FIFO Overflow 0b0..No occurrence of MB7 completing transmission or reception, or no FIFO overflow. 0b1..MB7 completed transmission or reception, or FIFO overflow.

◆ CAN_IFLAG1_BUF31TO8I [3/3]

#define CAN_IFLAG1_BUF31TO8I ( x)

BUF31TO8I - Buffer MBi Interrupt

◆ CAN_CTRL2_PES [2/2]

#define CAN_CTRL2_PES ( x)

PES - Payload Byte and Bit Order Selection 0b0..Big-endian 0b1..Little-endian

◆ CAN_CTRL2_ASD [2/2]

#define CAN_CTRL2_ASD ( x)

ASD - ACK Suppression Disable 0b0..Enabled 0b1..Disabled

◆ CAN_CTRL2_TSTAMPCAP

#define CAN_CTRL2_TSTAMPCAP ( x)

TSTAMPCAP - Timestamp Capture Point 0b00..Disabled 0b01..End of the CAN frame 0b10..Start of the CAN frame 0b11..Start of frame for classical CAN frames; res bit for CAN FD frames

◆ CAN_CTRL2_MBTSBASE

#define CAN_CTRL2_MBTSBASE ( x)

MBTSBASE - Message Buffer Timestamp Base 0b00..TIMER 0b01..Lower 16 bits of high-resolution timer 0b10..Upper 16 bits of high-resolution timer 0b11..Reserved

◆ CAN_CTRL2_EDFLTDIS [2/2]

#define CAN_CTRL2_EDFLTDIS ( x)

EDFLTDIS - Edge Filter Disable 0b0..Enabled 0b1..Disabled

◆ CAN_CTRL2_ISOCANFDEN [2/2]

#define CAN_CTRL2_ISOCANFDEN ( x)

ISOCANFDEN - ISO CAN FD Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_CTRL2_BTE [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_BTE ( x)

BTE - Bit Timing Expansion Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_CTRL2_PREXCEN [2/2]

#define CAN_CTRL2_PREXCEN ( x)

PREXCEN - Protocol Exception Enable 0b0..Disabled 0b1..Enabled

◆ CAN_CTRL2_TIMER_SRC

#define CAN_CTRL2_TIMER_SRC ( x)

TIMER_SRC - Timer Source 0b0..CAN bit clock 0b1..External time tick

◆ CAN_CTRL2_EACEN [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_EACEN ( x)

EACEN - Entire Frame Arbitration Field Comparison Enable for RX Message Buffers 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_CTRL2_RRS [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_RRS ( x)

RRS - Remote Request Storing 0b0..Generated 0b1..Stored

◆ CAN_CTRL2_MRP [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_MRP ( x)

MRP - Message Buffers Reception Priority 0b0..Matching starts from Legacy RX FIFO or Enhanced RX FIFO and continues on message buffers. 0b1..Matching starts from message buffers and continues on Legacy RX FIFO or Enhanced RX FIFO.

◆ CAN_CTRL2_TASD [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_TASD ( x)

TASD - Transmission Arbitration Start Delay

◆ CAN_CTRL2_RFFN [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_RFFN ( x)

RFFN - Number of Legacy Receive FIFO Filters

◆ CAN_CTRL2_BOFFDONEMSK [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_BOFFDONEMSK ( x)

BOFFDONEMSK - Bus Off Done Interrupt Mask 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_CTRL2_ERRMSK_FAST [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL2_ERRMSK_FAST ( x)

ERRMSK_FAST - Error Interrupt Mask for Errors Detected in the Data Phase of Fast CAN FD Frames 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_ESR2_IMB [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR2_IMB ( x)

IMB - Inactive Message Buffer 0b0..Message buffer indicated by ESR2[LPTM] is not inactive. 0b1..At least one message buffer is inactive.

◆ CAN_ESR2_VPS [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR2_VPS ( x)

VPS - Valid Priority Status 0b0..Invalid 0b1..Valid

◆ CAN_ESR2_LPTM [3/3]

#define CAN_ESR2_LPTM ( x)

LPTM - Lowest Priority TX Message Buffer

◆ CAN_CRCR_TXCRC [3/3]

#define CAN_CRCR_TXCRC ( x)

TXCRC - Transmitted CRC value

◆ CAN_CRCR_MBCRC [3/3]

#define CAN_CRCR_MBCRC ( x)

MBCRC - CRC Message Buffer

◆ CAN_RXFGMASK_FGM [3/3]

#define CAN_RXFGMASK_FGM ( x)

FGM - Legacy RX FIFO Global Mask Bits

◆ CAN_RXFIR_IDHIT [3/3]

#define CAN_RXFIR_IDHIT ( x)

IDHIT - Identifier Acceptance Filter Hit Indicator

◆ CAN_CBT_EPSEG2 [3/3]

#define CAN_CBT_EPSEG2 ( x)

EPSEG2 - Extended Phase Segment 2

◆ CAN_CBT_EPSEG1 [3/3]

#define CAN_CBT_EPSEG1 ( x)

EPSEG1 - Extended Phase Segment 1

◆ CAN_CBT_EPROPSEG [3/3]

#define CAN_CBT_EPROPSEG ( x)

EPROPSEG - Extended Propagation Segment

◆ CAN_CBT_ERJW [3/3]

#define CAN_CBT_ERJW ( x)

ERJW - Extended Resync Jump Width

◆ CAN_CBT_EPRESDIV [3/3]

#define CAN_CBT_EPRESDIV ( x)

EPRESDIV - Extended Prescaler Division Factor

◆ CAN_CBT_BTF [3/3]

#define CAN_CBT_BTF ( x)

BTF - Bit Timing Format Enable 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

◆ CAN_DBG1_CFSM [2/2]

#define CAN_DBG1_CFSM ( x)

CFSM - CAN Finite State Machine

◆ CAN_DBG1_CBN [2/2]

#define CAN_DBG1_CBN ( x)

CBN - CAN Bit Number

◆ CAN_DBG2_RMP [2/2]

#define CAN_DBG2_RMP ( x)

RMP - RX Matching Pointer

◆ CAN_DBG2_MPP [2/2]

#define CAN_DBG2_MPP ( x)

MPP - Matching Process in Progress 0b0..No matching process ongoing. 0b1..Matching process is in progress.

◆ CAN_DBG2_TAP [2/2]

#define CAN_DBG2_TAP ( x)

TAP - TX Arbitration Pointer

◆ CAN_DBG2_APP [2/2]

#define CAN_DBG2_APP ( x)

APP - Arbitration Process in Progress 0b0..No arbitration process ongoing. 0b1..Arbitration process is in progress.

◆ CAN_CS_TIME_STAMP [3/3]

#define CAN_CS_TIME_STAMP ( x)

TIME_STAMP - Free-Running Counter Time stamp. This 16-bit field is a copy of the Free-Running Timer, captured for Tx and Rx frames at the time when the beginning of the Identifier field appears on the CAN bus.

◆ CAN_CS_DLC [3/3]

#define CAN_CS_DLC ( x)

DLC - Length of the data to be stored/transmitted.

◆ CAN_CS_RTR [3/3]

#define CAN_CS_RTR ( x)

RTR - Remote Transmission Request. One/zero for remote/data frame.

◆ CAN_CS_IDE [3/3]

#define CAN_CS_IDE ( x)

IDE - ID Extended. One/zero for extended/standard format frame.

◆ CAN_CS_SRR [3/3]

#define CAN_CS_SRR ( x)

SRR - Substitute Remote Request. Contains a fixed recessive bit.

◆ CAN_CS_CODE [3/3]

#define CAN_CS_CODE ( x)

CODE - Message Buffer Code. This 4-bit field can be accessed (read or write) by the CPU and by the FlexCAN module itself, as part of the message buffer matching and arbitration process.

◆ CAN_CS_ESI [3/3]

#define CAN_CS_ESI ( x)

ESI - Error State Indicator. This bit indicates if the transmitting node is error active or error passive.

◆ CAN_CS_BRS [3/3]

#define CAN_CS_BRS ( x)

BRS - Bit Rate Switch. This bit defines whether the bit rate is switched inside a CAN FD format frame.

◆ CAN_CS_EDL [3/3]

#define CAN_CS_EDL ( x)

EDL - Extended Data Length. This bit distinguishes between CAN format and CAN FD format frames. The EDL bit must not be set for Message Buffers configured to RANSWER with code field 0b1010.

◆ CAN_ID_EXT [3/3]

#define CAN_ID_EXT ( x)

EXT - Contains extended (LOW word) identifier of message buffer.

◆ CAN_ID_STD [3/3]

#define CAN_ID_STD ( x)

STD - Contains standard/extended (HIGH word) identifier of message buffer.

◆ CAN_ID_PRIO [3/3]

#define CAN_ID_PRIO ( x)

PRIO - Local priority. This 3-bit fieldis only used when LPRIO_EN bit is set in MCR and it only makes sense for Tx buffers. These bits are not transmitted. They are appended to the regular ID to define the transmission priority.

DATA_BYTE_3 - Data byte 0 of Rx/Tx frame.

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PN_FCS [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PN_FCS ( x)

FCS - Filtering Combination Selection 0b00..Message ID filtering only 0b01..Message ID filtering and payload filtering 0b10..Message ID filtering occurring a specified number of times 0b11..Message ID filtering and payload filtering a specified number of times

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PN_IDFS [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PN_IDFS ( x)

IDFS - ID Filtering Selection 0b00..Match ID contents to an exact target value 0b01..Match an ID value greater than or equal to a specified target value 0b10..Match an ID value smaller than or equal to a specified target value 0b11..Match an ID value within a range of values, inclusive

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PN_PLFS [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PN_PLFS ( x)

PLFS - Payload Filtering Selection 0b00..Match payload contents to an exact target value 0b01..Match a payload value greater than or equal to a specified target value 0b10..Match a payload value smaller than or equal to a specified target value 0b11..Match upon a payload value within a range of values, inclusive

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PN_NMATCH [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PN_NMATCH ( x)

NMATCH - Number of Messages Matching the Same Filtering Criteria 0b00000001..Once 0b00000010..Twice 0b11111111..255 times

◆ CAN_CTRL1_PN_WUMF_MSK [3/3]

#define CAN_CTRL1_PN_WUMF_MSK ( x)

WUMF_MSK - Wake-up by Matching Flag Mask 0b0..Disable 0b1..Enable

#define RTC_CTRL2_TAMP_CFG_OVER ( x)

◆ RTC_CTRL2_WAKEUP_STATUS

#define RTC_CTRL2_WAKEUP_STATUS ( x)

WAKEUP_STATUS - Wakeup Status 0b00..The wakeup and hibernation pin is in HiZ mode. 0b01..The wakeup and hibernation pin is at logic 0. MCU is in sleep mode. 0b10..The wakeup and hibernation pin is at logic 1. MCU is in sleep mode. 0b11..

◆ RTC_CTRL2_WAKEUP_MODE

#define RTC_CTRL2_WAKEUP_MODE ( x)

WAKEUP_MODE - Wakeup Mode 0b0..Tamper pin 0 is used as the tamper pin. 0b1..Tamper pin 0 is used as a wakeup and hibernation pin.
